name: Google Workspace
description: Google's productivity suite (Gmail, Drive, Docs, Meet) with compliance evidence from the Admin console audit and investigation tool, Drive sharing reports, the security health page, the Admin SDK Reports API and BigQuery log exports.

steps.action: Sign in to the Google Admin console (admin.google.com) and go to Menu > Reporting > Audit and investigation > Admin log events. Set the date range to cover the collection period with no gap since the previous export
steps.permissions_required: Super Admin, or a delegated admin with the Audit and Investigation (Reports) privilege
steps.security_note: Use a delegated admin account with only the reporting privileges needed, not a shared Super Admin login
steps.audit_note: Log events are generated and timestamped by Google and cannot be edited by administrators; most log event data is retained for 6 months, so collect well within that window

steps.action: Switch the data source to Drive log events and add a filter/condition of Visibility change = External (or Visibility = Shared externally) to capture external file-sharing activity for the period
steps.permissions_required: Audit and Investigation administrator privilege for Drive log events
steps.audit_note: External-sharing events are the primary Drive evidence for data-sharing controls; note that Drive log events require an edition that includes Drive audit

steps.action: Export each filtered view using Export all — results are delivered to Google Sheets (export is limited to 100,000 rows, or 30 million rows with the security investigation tool). Name each export with the data source and date range
steps.audit_note: Download the Sheets export as CSV/PDF for the evidence pack and record the covered date range so period completeness can be demonstrated

steps.action: Go to Menu > Security > Security centre > Security health and capture the page showing the status of security settings (Drive sharing settings, automatic email forwarding, 2-Step Verification and so on), including any items flagged for attention
steps.permissions_required: Super Admin, or an admin with the Security Centre administrator privilege plus read access to users and organisational units
steps.audit_note: Changes to Admin console settings can take up to 24 hours to propagate to the security health page — capture it after settings changes have settled. Requires an edition with the security centre (e.g. Enterprise Standard/Plus)

steps.action: Upload the log exports and security health captures to the evidence repository with evidence ID references and the covered date ranges
steps.security_note: Review exports before upload — Drive log events include document titles and user email addresses; ensure the evidence repository access is appropriately restricted

L2.title: Google Workspace BigQuery Log Export (Built-In Scheduled)
L2.frequency: automated continuous export, weekly evidence extraction

steps.action: In the Google Cloud console, create (or designate) a BigQuery project and dataset to receive Workspace logs, and grant the Workspace administrator the BigQuery Admin (roles/bigquery.admin) and Project IAM Admin (roles/resourcemanager.projectIamAdmin) roles on the project
steps.permissions_required: Google Cloud project Owner or IAM admin; Google Workspace Super Admin for the Workspace side
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated project for compliance logs and restrict dataset access to the compliance team via IAM

steps.action: In the Admin console go to Menu > Reporting > Data integrations and tick 'Enable Google Workspace data export to Google BigQuery', selecting the target project and dataset. Requires Enterprise Standard/Plus, Education Standard/Plus, Frontline Standard/Plus or Enterprise Essentials Plus
steps.audit_note: Once enabled, Google exports the logs automatically on an ongoing basis (near-real-time, typically under 10 minutes) with no code or scheduler to maintain — this is the built-in scheduled export path

steps.action: Confirm data is arriving in the dataset's activity and usage tables (activity tables hold the audit log events; usage tables hold service usage data). The first data appears after the export is activated — it is not backfilled
steps.audit_note: Because the export is forward-only, enable it before the audit period you need to evidence begins

steps.action: Create saved queries for the evidence you need (e.g. Drive external-sharing events, admin setting changes) using Google's published example queries as a starting point, and export query results to the evidence repository on the collection cadence
steps.permissions_required: BigQuery Data Viewer plus BigQuery Job User on the logs dataset

steps.action: Document the export configuration (project, dataset, activation date, dataset ACLs) and file it with the evidence — it demonstrates the collection mechanism itself is controlled
steps.security_note: Review dataset IAM bindings quarterly; the tables contain user activity for the whole organisation

L3.title: Google Workspace Reports API Automated Collection

steps.action: In the Google Cloud console, create a dedicated service account, enable the Admin SDK API on its project and generate a key (or use workload identity federation to avoid long-lived keys)
steps.permissions_required: Google Cloud project Owner/Editor to create the service account
steps.security_note: Prefer workload identity federation over downloaded JSON keys; if a key is unavoidable, store it in a secrets manager and rotate it regularly

steps.action: In the Admin console go to Security > Access and data control > API controls > Domain-wide delegation and authorise the service account's client ID with the scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/admin.reports.audit.readonly only
steps.security_note: Grant only the read-only audit scope — domain-wide delegation with broad scopes is a common over-privilege finding
steps.audit_note: The delegation grant itself appears in Admin log events, evidencing controlled provisioning

steps.action: Schedule a daily job that impersonates an admin with the Reports privilege and calls GET https://admin.googleapis.com/admin/reports/v1/activity/users/all/applications/{applicationName} for the applications you need (admin, drive, token; login events belong to the identity provider's evidence), using startTime/endTime bounded to the collection window and handling nextPageToken pagination
steps.permissions_required: Impersonated admin with the Reports privilege; service account with domain-wide delegation

steps.action: Write the returned activity records to the evidence store as timestamped, immutable objects (e.g. write-once storage) named by application and date range
steps.audit_note: Keep the raw API JSON — it includes the Google-assigned event time and actor detail that gives the export its evidentiary value

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting: retry on 429/5xx with backoff, alert the compliance team on failed runs, and log every run's outcome and row counts
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection promptly; most log event data is retained about 6 months, but collecting daily avoids any dependence on retention limits

steps.action: Validate completeness daily — compare per-application event counts against a rolling baseline, and account for Google's documented data lag times (some applications' events can take hours to days to appear) by re-querying a trailing window
steps.audit_note: Re-querying a trailing window (e.g. the previous 3 days) guards against events that arrive late due to reporting lag

L4.title: Google Workspace Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: In the Admin console, enable sharing of Google Workspace data with Google Cloud services (Account settings > Legal and compliance > Sharing options) so Workspace audit logs flow into Cloud Logging in your Google Cloud organisation
steps.permissions_required: Super Admin (Workspace) and Organisation Administrator (Google Cloud)
steps.audit_note: Cloud Logging holds Workspace audit logs alongside Google Cloud audit logs, giving a single, centrally governed log estate

steps.action: Create an organisation-level Cloud Logging sink that routes the Workspace audit log entries continuously to Pub/Sub (for streaming into your SIEM) and/or to a locked BigQuery dataset or Cloud Storage bucket with a retention lock for immutable archive
steps.permissions_required: Logs Configuration Writer (roles/logging.configWriter) at the organisation level
steps.security_note: Grant the sink's writer identity access to the destination only; lock the archive bucket/dataset so pipeline operators cannot delete evidence
steps.audit_note: A bucket retention lock makes the archived logs demonstrably immutable, which materially strengthens their evidentiary value

steps.action: Keep the BigQuery Workspace export (Reporting > Data integrations) running in parallel as the analytical copy — its near-real-time activity tables are the source for completeness metrics and evidence queries

Build completeness metrics with scheduled BigQuery queries: events per day per application, arrival latency versus Google's published lag times, and coverage across organisational units; alert via Cloud Monitoring when a day's volume drops below baseline or the export stalls
steps.audit_note: Gap alerts and their resolution records are the evidence that collection is actively managed, not best-effort

steps.action: Publish a collection-health dashboard (events/day, latency, coverage, failed exports) and run an automated quarterly review that files a health report — including any gap incidents and the security health page trend — in the evidence repository
steps.audit_note: Pair the health report with the sink and export configuration exports to evidence both continuous collection and controlled configuration
